paper_title: Biofacies analysis and depositional environments of mid-Eocene larger benthic foraminifera-rich deposits in northern Tunisia

methods.description: Field geological investigations include bed-by-bed sampling with thorough observations of lithological and sedimentological features.

steps.description: Field geological investigations include bed-by-bed sampling with thorough observations of lithological and sedimentological features.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct bed-by-bed sampling and observe lithological and sedimentological features

methods.description: Laboratory analyses aimed at defining the petrographic texture of skeletal limestone beds and their micropaleontological content in LBF assemblages.

steps.description: A total of 33 samples were collected for thin-section observations under a 'Nikon Eclipse E 200' optical microscope for the identification of main skeletal and non-skeletal components, carbonate grains, matrix, and mineral compounds.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare thin sections and observe under optical microscope to identify components

steps.description: Components are divided into three categories, according to the relative abundance estimated under the optical microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Categorize components based on relative abundance

steps.description: Microphotographs were taken by means of a digital camera (Leica M80), transferred to the computer using a Nikon's Digital Sight DS-U3 microscope camera controller and treated with the imaging software Nikon NIS Elements F4.
steps.searchable_summary: Capture and process microphotographs using digital camera and imaging software

methods.description: The microfacies analysis and lithology description followed the classification of Flügel (2010) and Dunham (1962).

steps.description: To differentiate microfacies assemblages, all the allochem components and matrix were characterized and visually quantitatively estimated in thin sections.
steps.searchable_summary: Characterize and estimate allochem components and matrix in thin sections
